ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: In the past three months/past week, how often have you had thoughts about suicide
Notes: 0= Never; 10= Always

ElementDescription: In the past three months/past week, how much control have you had over these suicidal thoughts
Notes: 0= Never; 10= Always

ElementDescription: In the past three months/past week, how close have you come to making a suicide attempt?
Notes: 0= Never; 10= Always

ElementDescription: In the past three months/past week, to what extent have you felt tormented by thoughts about suicide
Notes: 0= Never; 10= Always

ElementDescription: In the past three months/past week, how much have thoughts about suicide interfered with your ability to carry out daily activities, such as work, household tasks or social activities
Notes: 0= Never; 10= Always

ElementDescription: SIDAS Sum score
Timepoint/visit label
Notes: e.g., Baseline, 6-Month Follow Up, Year 2; Morning, At Bedtime

ElementDescription: Please rate how helpful each of the following parts of the safety plan have been in helping you with suicidal thoughts on a scale of 1-4: ''contacting social supports''
Notes: 1 = Not at all helpful; 2 = A little helpful; 3 = Helpful; 4 = Very helpful; 77 = Legitimate skip (''no'' to sp_comp_ch'')
